description: In Hindi, retroflex stops (/ʈ, ʈʰ, ɖ, ɖʱ/) can have allophonic dental realizations [t̪, t̪ʰ, d̪, d̪ʱ] in vowel contexts, particularly before and after front vowels. This represents positional allophony within the retroflex series, where the constriction shifts forward depending on neighboring vowels.
sources: Sharma, A. (2009). _Retroflexion in Hindi: A Phonetic Study_. University of Texas at Austin.
Maddieson, I., & Ladefoged, P. (1993). _The phonetics of retroflex laterals_. UCLA Working Papers in Phonetics, 83, 111-119.
Kelkar, A. (2011). _Acoustic and Electropalatographic Study of Hindi Retroflex Stops_. Journal of Phonetics, 39(3), 468-478.
